scrs.name: SCRS Guide to Complete Repair Planning

operationTypes.R&I: Remove & Install/Reinstall - Remove, set aside, reinstall same part
operationTypes.R&R: Remove & Replace - Remove old, install new part
operationTypes.Add: Additional operation - Manual line added to estimate
operationTypes.O/H: Overhaul - Complete disassembly, repair, reassembly (HOURS VARY BY ESTIMATE - use database time)

laborTypes.B: Body Labor - Structural and panel work
laborTypes.F: Frame Labor - Structural correction on bench
laborTypes.G: Glass Labor - Windshield and glass installation

blendFormulas.description: Blend is added when refinishing panels adjacent to repaired/replaced panels for color match. IP default: 50% of full refinish time for two-stage, 70% for three-stage (CCC). SCRS Blend Study (2022) found blend actually takes 31.59% MORE time than full refinish on average.

ipDefaults.twoStage: 50% of full refinish time for that panel
ipDefaults.threeStage: 70% of full refinish time for that panel (CCC)
ipDefaults.example: 3.0 hr full refinish panel → 1.5 hr blend (two-stage) or 2.1 hr blend (three-stage)

scrsStudy.finding: Blend takes 31.59% MORE time than full refinish on average
scrsStudy.source: SCRS 2022 study with all 5 major refinish manufacturers

updatedGuidance.ccc: Added Edit Blend Rules — estimator can set custom blend value
updatedGuidance.audatex: Removed 50% formula, allows 50-150%, defers to estimator judgment

overlapDeductions.description: Overlap deductions apply when multiple adjacent panels are refinished

majorAdjacentPanel.notes: Deduct when refinishing adjacent major panels (e.g., fender AND door)

maskingFormulas.description: Masking time for openings and adjacent areas during refinish

commonLaborTimes.description: Typical labor times from real estimates (may vary by vehicle)

bodyOperations.notes: Manual entry - add when fog lamps present

commonLaborTimes.note: Database times (Repl, R&I, O/H) vary by vehicle - always use the estimating system times. The operations listed here are MANUAL ENTRY operations (#) that must be added separately.

manualEntryOperations.description: Operations marked with # in CCC are MANUAL ENTRIES - NOT INCLUDED in database times. Users must add these lines.

triCoatAdditive.description: Three-stage/tri-coat paint requires additional time per CCC/MOTOR G41
triCoatAdditive.formula: Add 20-30% to refinish time for 3-stage paint (CCC/MOTOR G41). CCC blend button calculates 3-stage blend at 70% of full refinish (vs 50% for two-stage).
triCoatAdditive.example: 3.0 hrs base refinish + 0.6-0.9 hr (20-30%) = 3.6-3.9 hrs total

orderOfOperations.description: Typical order of operations on a collision estimate

included: Removal of damaged quarter panel at factory seams (CCC/MOTOR)
Grinding, filling and final sanding with up to 150 grit sandpaper (Mitchell CEG)
Caulking/seam sealer to joints, gaps and seams for water sealing (CCC/MOTOR)
Use of inserts and sectioning per OEM/I-CAR guidelines (CCC/MOTOR)
Basic gap and fit alignment - ONE test fit only (CCC/MOTOR)
notIncluded: Weld-through primer application (Mitchell CEG - 'removal and/or application of weld-thru primers NOT INCLUDED')
E-Coat removal at weld flanges (DEG - protective coatings removal NOT INCLUDED)
Covering vehicle from weld spark damage (DEG Inquiry 13434)
Welding consumables: wire, gas, tips, coupons (CCC/MOTOR)
Additional seam sealer beyond attachment point (DEG Inquiry 23010)
Seam sealer as undercoating or sound deadening (CCC/MOTOR)
Seam sealer masking, setup, cleanup, test patterns (CCC/MOTOR)
Duplicating factory texture/color of seam sealer (CCC/MOTOR)
Second or additional test fits (CCC/MOTOR - only ONE test fit included)
Cavity wax / anti-corrosion treatment (Mitchell CEG G31)
Feather, Prime and Block operation (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
R&I of quarter glass (CCC Pathways - glass NOT INCLUDED)
R&I of tail light assembly (CCC Pathways)
R&I of fuel door/filler neck (CCC Pathways)
R&I of rear bumper cover (CCC Pathways)
R&I of wheel opening moldings (CCC Pathways)
Transfer of attached parts from old panel (Mitchell - transfer time NOT INCLUDED)
Refinish of new panel (Mitchell CEG - separate operation)
Blend to adjacent panels (Mitchell CEG Section 28 - NOT INCLUDED)
Pre-repair diagnostic scan (CCC/MOTOR - scan tool diagnostics NOT INCLUDED)
Post-repair diagnostic scan (CCC/MOTOR - scan tool diagnostics NOT INCLUDED)
ADAS calibration - blind spot monitor (CCC/MOTOR G33)
Measure/document vehicle structure (Mitchell CEG - NOT INCLUDED)
operations.scrsNotes: Per SCRS Guide to Complete Repair Planning: Welded panel replacement requires restoration of factory corrosion protection. Always check OEM procedures for specific requirements.

included: Grinding, filling and final sanding with up to 150 grit sandpaper (Mitchell CEG)
Caulking/seam sealer at welded joints for water sealing (CCC/MOTOR)
Basic gap and fit alignment - ONE test fit (CCC/MOTOR)
notIncluded: E-Coat removal at weld flanges (Mitchell CEG - protective coatings NOT INCLUDED)
Covering vehicle from weld spark damage (DEG Inquiry 13434)
Additional seam sealer beyond welded attachment (DEG Inquiry 23010)
Cavity wax / anti-corrosion inside rocker (Mitchell CEG G31)
R&I of rocker moldings/trim (CCC Pathways - moldings NOT INCLUDED)
R&I of seats for access (CCC Pathways)
Transfer of attached parts (Mitchell - transfer time NOT INCLUDED)
Feather, Prime and Block (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Blend to adjacent panels (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Pre/Post repair scans (CCC/MOTOR - diagnostics NOT INCLUDED)
operations.scrsNotes: SCRS: Critical structural component. Rocker is primary load path - always verify structural dimensions. Inspect for additional damage at A/B pillar connections.

included: Removal of damaged bumper cover (CCC/MOTOR G5)
Installation of new bumper cover (CCC/MOTOR G5)
Basic adjustment for fit - ONE test fit (CCC/MOTOR)
notIncluded: Transfer of fog lights (Mitchell - transfer time NOT INCLUDED)
Transfer of parking sensors (Mitchell - transfer time NOT INCLUDED)
Transfer of moldings/trim (CCC Pathways - moldings NOT INCLUDED)
Drill for license plate if new bumper (SCRS)
Adhesion promoter application (Mitchell CEG Section 22 - plastic NOT INCLUDED)
Flex additive in paint (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Feather, Prime and Block (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Clear coat (Mitchell CEG Section 28 - separate operation)
Color match/tinting (Mitchell CEG Section 28 - NOT INCLUDED)
Pre/Post repair scan (CCC/MOTOR - diagnostics NOT INCLUDED)
Excessive clip replacement due to collision damage (CCC/MOTOR G7)
operations.scrsNotes: SCRS: Check for ADAS components (radar, camera, sensors) that require calibration after bumper R&R. Reference OEM position statements for calibration triggers.

included: Basic bolt-up and adjustment - ONE test fit (CCC/MOTOR)
Reconnection of basic electrical connectors (CCC/MOTOR G4)
For REPAIR: Straighten minor damage, file, grind, sand (CCC/MOTOR G28)
notIncluded: R&I of fender liner (CCC Pathways - separate operation)
R&I of antenna if applicable (CCC Pathways)
R&I of side marker lights (CCC Pathways)
Transfer of moldings/emblems (Mitchell - transfer time NOT INCLUDED)
Feather, Prime and Block (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Refinish of new/repaired panel (Mitchell CEG - separate operation)
Blend to adjacent panels - door, hood (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Corrosion protection of hem flanges (Mitchell CEG G31)
operations.scrsNotes: SCRS: Check adjacent panels for damage. May require loosening bumper cover for proper fit. Blend operations to door and hood are typically required for color match.

included: Removal of damaged door assembly (CCC/MOTOR G5)
Installation of new door shell (CCC/MOTOR G5)
notIncluded: Transfer of door trim panel (Mitchell - transfer time NOT INCLUDED)
Transfer of window regulator (Mitchell - per Mitchell P-Pages for Door R&R)
Transfer of mirror (CCC Pathways - mirror NOT INCLUDED)
Transfer of belt moldings (CCC Pathways - moldings NOT INCLUDED)
Transfer of side impact beam if not included with door (Mitchell)
Feather, Prime and Block (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Blend to adjacent panels (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
operations.scrsNotes: SCRS: Always check door intrusion beam. Verify all electrical components function after transfer. R&I glass and regulator for door skin replacement is NOT INCLUDED per all three IPs.

notIncluded: Transfer of hood insulator (Mitchell - transfer time NOT INCLUDED)
Transfer of emblems/nameplates (CCC Pathways - emblems NOT INCLUDED)
Transfer of hood release cable attachment (Mitchell)
Feather, Prime and Block (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Blend to adjacent panels (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Clear coat and cut/polish (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
operations.scrsNotes: SCRS: Check hood hinges for damage. Verify hood latch operation after installation.

included: Removal of existing glass (Mitchell CEG G18)
notIncluded: Pinchweld repair (Mitchell CEG G18 - NOT INCLUDED)
Rust treatment of pinchweld (Mitchell CEG G18)
Primer application to damaged areas (Mitchell CEG G18)
R&I of windshield moldings (CCC Pathways - moldings NOT INCLUDED)
R&I of ADAS camera bracket (CCC Pathways)
ADAS camera calibration - CRITICAL (CCC/MOTOR G33 - NOT INCLUDED)
operations.scrsNotes: CRITICAL per SCRS: Forward-facing ADAS camera REQUIRES calibration after windshield replacement. This is safety-critical. Check all OEM position statements.

notIncluded: Transfer of bulbs (Mitchell - transfer time NOT INCLUDED)
Headlamp aiming (Mitchell CEG G19 - 'Headlight aiming NOT INCLUDED')
ADAS calibration if forward camera affected (CCC/MOTOR G33)
Module programming for adaptive headlights (CCC/MOTOR G21)
operations.scrsNotes: Per Mitchell G19: Headlamp aiming should be performed after headlight R&R or front-end collision repair. NOT INCLUDED in labor time.

included: Welding of new pillar per OEM/I-CAR guidelines (CCC/MOTOR)
Grinding, filling, sanding to 150 grit (Mitchell CEG)
Basic seam sealer at weld joints (CCC/MOTOR)
notIncluded: E-Coat removal (Mitchell CEG - protective coatings NOT INCLUDED)
R&I of airbag if applicable (CCC/MOTOR G14)
Frame measurement (Mitchell CEG G10 - 'measuring is NOT INCLUDED')
Feather, Prime and Block (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
operations.scrsNotes: Per SCRS: Structural component. May contain airbag components. Always verify frame dimensions per Mitchell G10.

included: Grinding, filling, sanding to 150 grit (Mitchell CEG)
Basic seam sealer at weld joints (CCC/MOTOR)
notIncluded: Seam sealer beyond weld joints (DEG Inquiry 23010)
R&I of rocker panel trim (CCC Pathways)
R&I of seat belt anchor (CCC/MOTOR G14 - SRS components)
R&I of seat belt retractor (CCC/MOTOR G14)
R&I of side curtain airbag (CCC/MOTOR G14 - 'SRS R&I NOT INCLUDED')
Feather, Prime and Block (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
operations.scrsNotes: Per SCRS: B-Pillar contains seat belt anchors and may house side curtain airbags. Critical structural component. SRS inspection required per CCC/MOTOR G14.

included: Grinding, filling, sanding to 150 grit (Mitchell CEG)
Basic seam sealer at weld joints (CCC/MOTOR)
notIncluded: E-Coat removal at weld flanges (Mitchell CEG)
Seam sealer beyond weld joints (DEG Inquiry 23010)
R&I of sunroof assembly if equipped (CCC Pathways)
R&I of all glass - windshield, back glass (CCC/MOTOR G18)
Feather, Prime and Block entire roof (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Blend to adjacent panels (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
operations.scrsNotes: Per SCRS: Major structural repair. Verify pillars and rails are not damaged before roof replacement. Frame measurement required per Mitchell G10.

included: NONE - All diagnostic operations are NOT INCLUDED per CCC/MOTOR G9
notIncluded: Pre-repair diagnostic scan (CCC/MOTOR G9 - explicitly NOT INCLUDED)
Scan tool clear/reset electronic module (CCC - listed as not-included)
operations.scrsNotes: Per CCC/MOTOR G9: Many OEMs recommend pre/post-repair diagnostic scans on all vehicles involved in a collision. Time should be based on actual shop time. Neither CCC nor Mitchell publish specific scan times - Audatex has begun publishing scan labor times.

included: NONE - ADAS calibration is NOT INCLUDED per CCC/MOTOR G33
notIncluded: Road test for dynamic calibration (CCC/MOTOR G33)
triggers: Front bumper R&R or R&I with radar (CCC/MOTOR G33)
Rear bumper R&R or R&I with parking sensors (CCC/MOTOR G33)
Mirror R&R with blind spot system (CCC/MOTOR G33)
operations.scrsNotes: Per CCC/MOTOR G33: ADAS calibration is required after many common collision repairs. Always check OEM requirements and position statements. Safety-critical operation.

included: NONE - Corrosion protection is NOT INCLUDED per CCC/MOTOR G31
notIncluded: Weld-through primer (Mitchell CEG - 'removal and/or application of weld-thru primers NOT INCLUDED')
Seam sealer application beyond weld joints (CCC/MOTOR G31)
Remove/apply wax, petroleum based coatings (Mitchell CEG)
Time to free parts frozen by rust or corrosion (Mitchell CEG)
operations.scrsNotes: Per CCC/MOTOR G31 and Mitchell: Corrosion protection should be applied to all bare metal, weld areas, and enclosed cavities. This is NEVER included in labor times.

included: Scuff panel and clean (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Mask adjacent panels up to 36 inches (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Prime or seal as required (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Final sanding and clean (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
notIncluded: Blending into adjacent panels (Mitchell CEG Section 28 - NOT INCLUDED)
Color match or tinting (Mitchell CEG Section 28 - NOT INCLUDED)
Anti-corrosion rust resistant material (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Soft chip primers or anti-chip undercoat (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Finish sand and buff (Mitchell CEG Section 28 - NOT INCLUDED)
Vehicle bagging when required (Mitchell - add 0.2 hour per application)
Interior masking to prevent overspray (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Release agent removal from raw plastic (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Feather, Prime & Block welded panels (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Gravel guard refinish (Mitchell - 0.5 first panel, 0.3 each additional)
Cost of paint and materials (Mitchell - NEVER included)
operations.scrsNotes: Per Mitchell CEG Section 28: Refinish times are for NEW, UNDAMAGED PARTS without trim or attached components. The cost of paint and materials is NEVER included in refinish time.

included: NONE - Wheel alignment is NOT INCLUDED per CCC/MOTOR G15
notIncluded: Wheel alignment - 2 or 4 wheel (CCC/MOTOR G15 - NOT INCLUDED)
operations.scrsNotes: Per CCC/MOTOR G15: Wheel alignment should be performed after any steering/suspension work. Always a separate, not-included operation.

included: O-ring replacement at disconnect points (CCC/MOTOR G17)
System evacuation (Mitchell CEG - included in some operations)
notIncluded: Refrigerant recovery/evacuation (CCC/MOTOR G17 - may be NOT INCLUDED)
operations.scrsNotes: Per CCC/MOTOR G17: Refrigerant recovery is required by law. A/C system should be leak tested before recharge. Receiver/drier replacement is typically required when system is open to atmosphere.

notIncluded: Transfer of latch/striker (Mitchell - transfer time NOT INCLUDED)
Feather, Prime and Block (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Blend to adjacent panels (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
operations.scrsNotes: Check for rear camera, parking sensors, and power liftgate components that require calibration.

included: Removal of damaged trunk lid (CCC/MOTOR G5)
Installation of new trunk lid (CCC/MOTOR G5)
notIncluded: Transfer of latch (Mitchell - transfer time NOT INCLUDED)
Feather, Prime and Block (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
Blend to adjacent panels (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
operations.scrsNotes: Check for rear camera and parking sensors.

notIncluded: Transfer of mirror glass (Mitchell - transfer time NOT INCLUDED)
Refinish if body color (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
ADAS calibration - blind spot monitor (CCC/MOTOR G33)
operations.scrsNotes: Check for blind spot monitoring, lane change assist, and 360 camera systems.

notIncluded: Transfer of emblems (Mitchell - transfer time NOT INCLUDED)
operations.scrsNotes: Active grille shutters and front radar commonly located behind grille.

notIncluded: R&I of cooling system components (CCC Pathways)
operations.scrsNotes: Structural component - verify frame dimensions.

included: Removal of damaged tail light (CCC/MOTOR G5)
Installation of new tail light (CCC/MOTOR G5)
notIncluded: Transfer of bulbs (Mitchell - transfer time NOT INCLUDED)
operations.scrsNotes: Check for blind spot monitoring sensors that may be integrated.
Often damaged but overlooked. Check for damage during wheel well inspection.

notIncluded: R&I of brake components for access (CCC Pathways)
operations.scrsNotes: Always verify TPMS function after wheel work. Check for wheel bearing damage after collision.
Wheel alignment required after ANY suspension work. SAS reset needed on most modern vehicles.

included: Application of adhesive per OEM procedure (CCC/MOTOR G6)
notIncluded: Adhesive curing time (DEG 14014 CCC - wait time NOT considered in motor time)
Monitoring curing with IR lamps (DEG 12709 Mitchell - NOT INCLUDED)
Adhesive curing times (DEG 12953 Audatex - Section 4-2 Labor Exclusions)
operations.scrsNotes: Per DEG Inquiries 12709, 12953, 14014: All three information providers confirm adhesive curing/wait time is NOT INCLUDED. OEM procedures may require specific cure times before vehicle can proceed.

included: NONE - Paint thickness measurement is NOT INCLUDED per DEG inquiries
notIncluded: Measuring MIL thickness of paint (DEG 19625 CCC - NOT INCLUDED)
Materials for ultrasonic couplant gels (DEG 19625 CCC)
Labor to create radial templates (DEG 19625 CCC)
Measuring material thickness (DEG 14513 Mitchell - NOT factored into labor times)
operations.scrsNotes: DEG 19625 & 14513: Paint thickness measurement is NOT INCLUDED in any estimating system. Required by some OEMs before refinish to verify substrate condition.

included: ONE disable and enable cycle per OEM procedure (DEG 17882)
Battery disconnection for SRS work (CCC/MOTOR G14)
notIncluded: Additional disable/enable cycles (DEG 17882 - requires agreement among parties)
SRS component R&I (CCC/MOTOR G14 - NOT INCLUDED)
Pre/Post repair scan for SRS codes
operations.scrsNotes: DEG 17882: Labor allowance includes all steps ONE time only. Multiple disable/enable cycles during repair require additional agreed-upon time.

notIncluded: Multiple disconnections throughout repair (DEG 16158 - requires evaluation)
operations.scrsNotes: DEG 16158: Battery disconnect included ONE time. Multiple disconnections during multi-stage repairs require on-the-spot evaluation.

included: ONE HV system disable per OEM procedure
notIncluded: Duplicate disable procedures throughout repair stages (DEG 16931 Tesla)
operations.scrsNotes: DEG 16931 (Tesla Model 3): Multiple HV disable procedures during repair stages are NOT INCLUDED. EV/Hybrid repairs require certified technicians.

included: Basic installation of recycled assembly (same as new part time)
notIncluded: Transfer of components TO recycled part
Repair of minor damage on recycled part
operations.scrsNotes: DEG 16546: When using recycled assemblies, component removal operations are NOT INCLUDED. Add time for prep, cleaning, and transfers.

included: NONE - Spray out operations are NOT INCLUDED per DEG 16149
notIncluded: Spray test panels done separately from vehicle (DEG 16149)
operations.scrsNotes: DEG 16149: Spray out cards/panels done separately from vehicle refinishing are NOT INCLUDED. Essential for proper color matching.

included: Basic coolant top-off after component R&R
notIncluded: Filling and bleeding cooling systems - certain operations (DEG 40223)
ATF fluid checking/filling after radiator service (DEG 17115)
operations.scrsNotes: DEG 40223/17115: Certain cooling system operations are NOT INCLUDED. Check ATF level if transmission cooler lines were disturbed.

included: NONE - Additional technician labor is NOT INCLUDED per DEG 16414
operations.scrsNotes: DEG 16414: Labor times are developed for ONE technician. When OEM procedures or part size require assistance, evaluate on-the-spot.

included: Masking adjacent panels up to 36 inches (Mitchell CEG Section 28)
operations.scrsNotes: DEG 17278: Masking items WITHIN panel perimeter (fuel doors, vents, openings) is NOT INCLUDED in standard refinish time.

included: Removal of damaged door skin (CCC/MOTOR G5)
Installation of new door skin (CCC/MOTOR G6)
operations.scrsNotes: DEG 16087: Door frame straightening during door skin replacement is NOT INCLUDED. Door structure must be correct before skin installation.
DEG 17327: Frameless door windows may require additional adjustment time beyond standard alignment for proper fit and seal.
DEG 40215: Seam sealer application on bumper reinforcement is NOT INCLUDED per Solera/Audatex.

notIncluded: Removal of adhesive wheel opening moldings during overhaul (DEG 16513 Audi Q5)
operations.scrsNotes: DEG 16513: Adhesive-attached moldings require additional time for proper removal without damage. Adhesive material is NOT INCLUDED.
DEG 15563: Creating positioning templates for emblems/nameplates is NOT INCLUDED. Critical for proper alignment of individual letter nameplates.

included: Standard refinish at natural break points (body lines, edges)
notIncluded: Creating blend lines at non-break points on welded panels (DEG 17409)
operations.scrsNotes: DEG 17409: Creating blend/break lines at locations other than natural body line break points is NOT INCLUDED in standard refinish time.

items: Welding consumables: wire, gas, tips, coupons
Seam sealer beyond weld attachment points (DEG 23010)
Release agent removal from raw plastic

allRefinish.source: Mitchell CEG Section 28, CCC/MOTOR G34-G41, DEG Inquiries
items: Feather, Prime and Block new parts (Mitchell - NOT INCLUDED)
Blend to adjacent panels (Mitchell - NOT INCLUDED)
Creating blend lines at non-break points (DEG 17409)
Wet sand and buff (CCC/MOTOR G39 - NOT INCLUDED)
Cost of paint and materials (NEVER included)
Transfer of attached parts from old to new
Transfer of welded/riveted/bonded brackets (Mitchell - NOT INCLUDED)
Replacing or transfer of mounting studs (except with specific footnote)
Duplicate disable procedures through repair stages
Door frame straightening before skin install (DEG 16087)

12709.response: Regarding cure adhesive with IR lamps and temperature monitoring: Monitoring curing and temperature is NOT INCLUDED in published labor allowances.

12953.response: Per Database Reference Manual Section 4-2 Labor Exclusions (page 43): Adhesive curing times is NOT INCLUDED.

13434.response: Covering the exterior, interior and components of the vehicle to prevent weld spark damage is NOT INCLUDED with welded panel replacement estimated work times. Requires on-the-spot evaluation.

14014.response: The Motor labor time in CCC One is for the actual time the technician spends on the physical procedure required to complete repairs. Wait time (i.e. drying, curing, shipping) is NOT considered in motor time.

14513.response: Measuring material thickness is NOT factored into the development of labor times.

15563.response: Creating templates and material costs for template fabrication are NOT INCLUDED in transfer or installation times.

16087.response: Straightening door frame during door skin replacement or repairs post-installation are NOT INCLUDED in door skin replacement time.

16149.response: Spray test panels/cards done separately from vehicle refinishing are NOT INCLUDED. Add time for color verification spray-outs.

16158.response: D&R of battery is included ONE time. Multiple disconnections throughout repair process require on-the-spot evaluation for additional time.

16414.response: Labor for additional technicians assisting with repair tasks (holding panels, supporting components) is NOT INCLUDED. Evaluate on-the-spot.

16513.response: Removal of adhesive wheel opening moldings during overhaul operations is NOT INCLUDED in panel overhaul time.

16546.response: Component removal operations when serviced with recycled assembly are NOT INCLUDED. Add R&I time for components on used parts.

16931.response: Duplicate disable procedures throughout repair stages are NOT INCLUDED. Multiple HV disable/enable cycles require additional time.

17115.response: Checking and filling ATF fluid after radiator service or cooler line disconnection is NOT INCLUDED.

17278.response: Masking for fuel doors, glass openings, vents, and other items within panel perimeter is NOT INCLUDED in refinish time.

17327.response: Additional adjustments beyond normal alignment for correct fit on frameless door windows is NOT INCLUDED.

17409.response: Creating blend lines at non-break points on welded panels is NOT INCLUDED in standard refinish time.

17882.response: Labor allowance for Disable & Enable Air Bag System includes all steps ONE time. If additional Disable & Enable is required, time should be agreed upon among all parties.

19625.response: Measuring the MIL thickness of paint is NOT INCLUDED in estimated work time. Materials for ultrasonic couplant gels and labor to create radial templates are also NOT INCLUDED.

23010.response: The estimated work time INCLUDES replacing all seam sealer that was removed due to removal of part. Any ADDITIONAL seam sealer replacement would need to be an on-the-spot evaluation.

23296.response: Provides feedback on adjacent panel damage for welded panel replacement.

40215.response: Per Solera Database Reference Manual Section 4-2, Application of Seam Sealer is listed under Labor Exclusions - NOT INCLUDED.

40223.response: Certain operations when filling and bleeding cooling systems are NOT INCLUDED per MOTOR feedback. Reference Guide To Estimating: RADIATOR section.
